contextSummary: Dify's design-system Drawer is the only wrapper in this set using `Drawer.Indent`/`Drawer.IndentBackground` — the parts that visually recede an already-open drawer when a second, nested drawer opens on top of it — with per-`swipeDirection` Tailwind transform classes for all four edges.
rankRationale: The only wrapper using Drawer.Indent/IndentBackground — the parts that recede an already-open drawer behind a newly-opened nested one — with all four swipeDirections supported via data-attribute-driven transforms. Feeds the Anatomy section's Indent/IndentBackground documentation and a 'nested drawers' story.

contextSummary: A real production site's mobile navigation drawer: `swipeDirection='left'`, an edge `SwipeArea` sibling to the trigger, `Backdrop`, and a `Popup` holding a full nav list plus a theme toggle — the canonical 'off-canvas mobile nav' use case for Drawer, distinct from every settings/registry example in this set.
rankRationale: The canonical off-canvas mobile-navigation use case, live on a real site: SwipeArea as an edge-swipe affordance, Backdrop, and a Popup holding the full nav list plus a theme toggle. Directly recomposable as the project's 'mobile nav drawer' real-world story — the single most relatable Drawer use case for most readers.

contextSummary: A grocery/pantry-tracking app's 'pantry drawer': uses `Drawer.createHandle()` (multi-instance drawer coordination), `swipeDirection='right'`, and wires `onOpenChange` to reset a `react-hook-form` form to the latest store state whenever the drawer reopens — a genuine real-feature example combining Drawer with external form/state libraries.
rankRationale: Combines Drawer.createHandle() (multi-instance coordination) with react-hook-form, resetting the form to the latest store snapshot on every reopen via onOpenChange — a genuine real-feature composition of Drawer with external form/state libraries, not just a styled shell.

contextSummary: An S3-bucket-management platform's UI library wraps Drawer into a `position`-aware compound component (left/right/top/bottom, each mapped to the matching `swipeDirection`) with iOS-Safari floating-bar height compensation — and ships its **own `drawer.stories.tsx`**, i.e. a peer project already doing exactly the kind of Storybook authoring this project is doing for Base UI's Drawer.
rankRationale: A position-aware compound wrapper (left/right/top/bottom, each mapped to the matching swipeDirection) with iOS Safari floating-bar height compensation, and — notably — its own drawer.stories.tsx: a peer project already writing Storybook stories for Base UI's Drawer, valuable prior art for this project's own story authoring.

contextSummary: Propel is Plane's (a real, well-known open-source Linear/Asana alternative project-management tool) own component library, and it has a *published Storybook* at the homepage URL — directly relevant prior art for this project's own Storybook effort. Its Drawer wrapper is a minimal 1:1 behavior-only re-export with an explicit architecture comment: 'behavior-only role that renders no element of its own... lives in components; the styled parts live in elements/drawer'.
rankRationale: Propel is Plane's (a well-known open-source PM tool) own component library, with a *published Storybook* at its homepage URL — directly relevant prior art. Its Drawer file is a clean 1:1 behavior-only re-export with an explicit code comment on the behavior-vs-styled-parts split, useful to cite in a composition/architecture note.

contextSummary: The broadest single-repo example set found for Drawer: nine separate demo files (basic, top/left/bottom, nested, non-modal, scrolling, settings, swipe-area) documenting nearly every Drawer configuration axis in one place, including the rarely-used `Indent`/`IndentBackground` parts for nested-drawer depth cues.
rankRationale: The broadest single-repo example breadth found: nine demo files covering nearly every configuration axis (direction, nesting, non-modal, scrolling, settings) in one place, including the rare Indent/IndentBackground nested-drawer parts. A rich source for multiple story variants at once.

contextSummary: Ships both a `drawer.tsx` and a shadcn-convention `sheet.tsx` — two names for the same Base UI Dialog-based edge panel. This pass additionally found a real *consumer* of the pattern inside coss's own examples app: a Cal.com-style settings drawer, i.e. one repo showing both the registry component and an example of it wired into a real feature.
rankRationale: Ships both drawer.tsx and a shadcn-convention sheet.tsx for the same component, and — uniquely in this set — also has a real *consumer* of the pattern in its own examples app (a Cal.com-style settings drawer), showing both the registry item and a feature using it.

contextSummary: The same drawer/sheet dual-naming pattern as coss, with a parallel Radix-backed 'radix-*' style registry shipped alongside the Base UI 'base-*' one in the same repo — a rare same-repo Base-UI-vs-Radix side-by-side comparison point.
rankRationale: Same drawer/sheet dual-naming pattern as coss, but notably ships a parallel Radix-backed 'radix-*' registry style alongside the Base UI 'base-*' one in the same repo — a rare same-repo side-by-side Base-UI-vs-Radix comparison point worth citing in a migration/decision-log note.

contextSummary: Notably has **not** built a styled Drawer wrapper yet — it ships only the auto-generated `primitives/drawer.ts` re-export (`export * from "@base-ui/react/drawer"`), the same honest 'not skinned yet' signal already observed for Cloudflare's own navigation-menu primitive.
rankRationale: An honest 'not skinned yet' signal: Cloudflare's own design system re-exports Drawer verbatim with no styling at all, mirroring the same pattern already seen on their navigation-menu primitive — useful as a citation that even sophisticated adopters sometimes ship the bare primitive first.

contextSummary: Base UI components for React (shadcn-style) — fixes Drawer, Select, Toast and overlay/focus issues. Description-based signal only; no cached subpath search or file inspection has verified an actual Drawer file in this repo.
rankRationale: Weakest-evidence entry: description-only signal from an earlier pass, never confirmed by a code search or file inspection. Kept for completeness at the bottom of the ranking; re-verify before citing.
